source: Frontier-authored upper tier for the tiered-ontology grounding (topic-tier.ts). Grounded in the KBpedia KKO upper ontology (Charles S. Peirce universal categories) per canon/kko-alignment.json. KKO/KBpedia is CC-BY-4.0; the descriptions here are authored by the frontier model, not the local 7B.
note: The UPPER tier of the three-layer space. SURJECTION: every domain's general (middle) topic is covered by a domain anchor; the anchors in turn roll up to the three KKO universal categories. The middle/general node for a domain is named 'general <domain>' — the connective tissue the candidate provider synthesizes and that lower (specific) topics inject into.

kko_categories.label: Firstness — possibility / quality
kko_categories.description: Pure possibility, quality, feeling, potential — a property considered in itself before it is instantiated or related. Qualities, capacities, ranges of value, the merely-possible.

kko_categories.label: Secondness — particular / instance / reaction
kko_categories.description: Actual individuals, concrete instances, events, measurements, brute facts and reactions — a thing here and now, a specific value, an observed outcome, a force acting against another.

kko_categories.label: Thirdness — general / law / type / mediation
kko_categories.description: Laws, types, general rules, habits, mediation, theories and regularities that govern particulars — the equations, principles, and classifications that hold across instances. The home of scientific law and abstraction.

domain_anchors.label: Physics
domain_anchors.description: The study of matter, energy, motion, force, and their interactions through space and time: mechanics, energy and momentum, electricity and magnetism, waves, heat, light, relativity, and quantum behaviour, governed by mathematical physical law.

domain_anchors.label: Mathematics
domain_anchors.description: The abstract science of number, quantity, structure, space, and change: arithmetic, algebra, geometry, calculus, probability, and proof — formal systems of definitions, theorems, and deductive reasoning.

domain_anchors.label: Chemistry
domain_anchors.description: The science of substances — atoms, elements, molecules, bonds, reactions, energy and equilibria: composition, structure, properties, and transformation of matter at the atomic and molecular scale.

domain_anchors.label: Biology
domain_anchors.description: The science of living systems: cells, genetics and heredity, evolution, physiology, ecology, molecular and cellular processes, organisms and their interactions with the environment.

domain_anchors.label: Computer Science
domain_anchors.description: The study of computation, algorithms, data structures, programming, complexity, systems, and information: how problems are represented, solved efficiently, and executed on machines.

domain_anchors.label: Economics
domain_anchors.description: The social science of production, distribution, and consumption of goods and services: supply and demand, prices and markets, incentives, money, growth, and the allocation of scarce resources by individuals, firms, and governments.

domain_anchors.label: Medicine
domain_anchors.description: The science and practice of human health and disease: anatomy and physiology, pathology, diagnosis, pharmacology, and treatment of illness across the body's systems.
